codeReview.summary: The commit correctly replaces fixed derived-data paths with per-invocation mktemp directories, but it omits creation of the `.derived-data` parent directory before calling `mktemp -d`. On clean environments where `.derived-data` does not already exist, all three updated scripts fail immediately, so the hardening is not reliably runnable.

issues.description: The script now calls `mktemp -d "$repo_root/.derived-data/openburnbar-app-tests.XXXXXX"` before ensuring `$repo_root/.derived-data` exists. `mktemp` fails when the parent directory is missing, causing test startup failure on fresh clones or cleaned workspaces.

Same parent-directory precondition bug as app tests: `mktemp -d "$repo_root/.derived-data/openburnbar-retrieval-evals.XXXXXX"` is executed without creating `$repo_root/.derived-data` first, so the script is not robust in clean environments.
Same parent-directory precondition bug in lock check script: `mktemp -d "$repo_root/.derived-data/openburnbar-lock-check.XXXXXX"` assumes `.derived-data` already exists and exits non-zero otherwise.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker verification demonstrates rerun isolation only in an environment where `.derived-data` already existed, so clean-workspace startup behavior was not exercised.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton entry for session 6f7eda80-6fca-45e5-958e-af0a9a4c3a7c shows repeated `scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh` runs and post-run glob checks, but no setup/validation step that removes or recreates `.derived-data` from scratch.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton entry, and commit diff for misc-harden-test-deriveddata-isolation. Verdict is fail: three updated scripts now rely on a pre-existing `.derived-data` parent and can fail on clean environments, leaving the derived-data isolation hardening incomplete.
